mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a contraction in the industrial production index reflects constrained output capacity and margin compression, forcing equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles do not predict negative 20-day returns, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital for highly leveraged firms rises sharply, triggering immediate deleveraging and short-term equity sell-offs over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect is indistinguishable from random noise (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor demand absorption, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that drives positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity earnings forecasts, producing a consistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward monthly returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index greater than 0.5% signals acute supply-side disruption, prompting immediate risk aversion and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following an INDPRO drop exceeding 0.5%, or the signal accuracy does not exceed 52% in walk-forward validation.
